title: papaver corsicum
intro: Papaver corsicum, also known as the Corn Poppy or Shirley Poppy, is an annual flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are due to the presence of alkaloids such as protopine and sanguinarine. The health benefits of Papaver corsicum include its ability to alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds, as well as its use as a diuretic to treat urinary issues. Herbal preparations of Papaver corsicum can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat respiratory and urinary problems.
